idea-plugin.name: Optional Converter
idea-plugin.description:
Convert Getter Chains to Optional

Wraps chained no-arg method calls in Optional to eliminate NullPointerException.

Before

String value = a.getB().getC().getValue();

After

String value = Optional.ofNullable(a)
        .map(A::getB)
        .map(B::getC)
        .map(C::getValue)
        .orElse(null);

Features

  • Intention action — place the caret on a getter chain, press Alt+Enter, select Convert to Optional chain
  • Refactor menu — Refactor → Convert to Optional Chain
  • Smart nullability — uses Optional.of() when the root is provably non-null (new T(), this, @NotNull, final-field initializers) and Optional.ofNullable() otherwise
  • Method references — generates A::getB style references; falls back to lambdas when the type cannot be resolved
  • Auto-import — adds import java.util.Optional automatically

Supported patterns

  • a.getB().getC() — explicit root
  • getA().getB().getC() — implicit root (first call becomes ofNullable argument)
  • a.getB(x).getC().getD() — converts only the no-arg tail
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.1

  • Supported IDE range extended to 2021.3+
  • Improved nullability analysis: effectively final local variables now recognized as non-null

1.0.0

  • Initial release
  • Intention action and Refactor menu integration
  • Smart of() vs ofNullable() based on nullability analysis
  • Method reference generation with lambda fallback
